note: Day 35 (Friday). WARM PIPELINE LINKEDIN BUFFER CONFIRMED EXPIRED TODAY (2026-05-02). Leads 1/3/22 (Tom/Claire/Rob) are now eligible for LinkedIn outreach. Preferred execution: TUESDAY 2026-05-06 — the first working day after UK May Day bank holiday (Monday 2026-05-05). DATE CORRECTION from prior runs: previous runs cited 'Monday 2026-05-04' as the execution date — this was a drafting error. May 4 is Sunday (weekend, blocked). May 5 is bank holiday Monday (blocked). The first viable working day is TUESDAY 2026-05-06. This fortuitously converges with the Batch 5 initial email send date — both channels open simultaneously on the post-bank-holiday Tuesday that historically produced all 3 INTERESTED replies in this campaign. No Batch 5 Ryedale data received — no new leads to query. Zero confirmed LinkedIn connections or notes sent across 35 campaign days.
orchestrator_status: NOT FOUND — src/outlocal/linkedin/orchestrator.py does not exist. All LinkedIn execution is manual (HANDS).

bank_holiday_linkedin_rule.rule: NO LinkedIn outreach on UK bank holidays. Business owners are offline — acceptance rate negligible and a connection request on a bank holiday reads as automated. BLOCKED.
bank_holiday_linkedin_rule.weekend_rule: Saturday 2026-05-03 and Sunday 2026-05-04 also blocked per no_linkedin_on_weekends rule.
bank_holiday_linkedin_rule.date_correction_note: Prior runs (Day 25 through Day 34) consistently referenced 'Monday 2026-05-04' as the preferred LinkedIn execution date. Corrected: May 4, 2026 is a Sunday — not a Monday. May 5 is the bank holiday. TUESDAY 2026-05-06 is the correct preferred execution date.
bank_holiday_linkedin_rule.strategic_upside: Post-bank-holiday Tuesday mirrors the exact conditions of Tuesday 2026-04-07 — the first working day after Easter Monday, which produced all 3 INTERESTED email replies. Connection requests on a Tuesday after a long weekend may benefit from the same 'returning-to-work, settling in' attention pattern. No guarantee, but the timing is structurally optimal.

email_outreach_state_check.imap_status: CRITICAL OVERDUE — 28 days. HANDS must sweep entire cohort before any LinkedIn action.

leads_emailed_3_plus_days_no_reply.note: All Batch 1-4 leads (~60) were emailed 3+ days ago, but their Day 3-6 LinkedIn window has definitively expired — sequences complete at Day-14 breakup. Warm pipeline leads 1/3/22 represent the only active LinkedIn candidates: their 7-day post-close buffer has expired today.
leads_emailed_3_plus_days_no_reply.warm_pipeline_status: Buffer confirmed expired today (2026-05-02). LinkedIn available from today. Preferred: TUESDAY 2026-05-06.

eligibility_check.rule: LinkedIn connection window for cold email leads: Day 3-6 after initial email. Warm pipeline nurture: after 7-day post-close buffer expires AND IMAP confirms no reply.
eligibility_check.imap_precondition: MANDATORY — 28-day IMAP gap. Sweep IMAP for each lead BEFORE sending any connection request. A reply (including rejection, conversion, or any communication since 2026-04-04) permanently closes LinkedIn for that lead.
eligibility_check.batch_5_window_status: NOT APPLICABLE — no initial Batch 5 sends made. LinkedIn Day 3-6 window begins after Batch 5 sends fire. If sends fire Tuesday 2026-05-06: Day 3 = Friday 2026-05-09.
eligibility_check.batch_1_4_window_status: CLOSED — all sequences complete. Day 3-6 window expired for all ~60 leads.

search_queries.name: Tom
search_queries.linkedin_sector_priority: low — trades, first name only. Attempt because lead showed genuine intent (pricing question, acknowledged workflow pain).
search_queries.fallback_query_1: site:linkedin.com/in carpentry joinery "Ripon" "North Yorkshire" owner director
search_queries.profile_verification_required: Confirm profile is clearly the owner/operator of Ripon Road Carpentry in Ripon before connecting. Do NOT connect to a generic 'Tom' in carpentry elsewhere in Yorkshire.
search_queries.do_not_send_if: IMAP reveals any email reply since 2026-04-04 — including after the final close email
search_queries.note_type_on_find: post_reference if post found within last 60 days — write bespoke note. If no recent post: use generic note from linkedin_connection_notes.json.
search_queries.brain_status: PRESUMED_LOST_NO_RESPONSE — INTERESTED reply 2026-04-04 (pricing question, acknowledged quote-form pain). LinkedIn is last available soft channel before archiving.
search_queries.seasonal_context: May-August is peak spring/summer carpentry season — Tom may simply have been too busy to reply. The pain point (no quote form) is structural and has not gone away.

search_queries.name: Claire
search_queries.linkedin_sector_priority: high — salons typically active on LinkedIn and social media. Post_reference upgrade expected.
search_queries.fallback_query_1: site:linkedin.com/in hairdresser salon owner director "Leeds" "West Yorkshire"
search_queries.fallback_query_2: site:linkedin.com/in "Westfield Hair & Beauty" owner manager Leeds
search_queries.profile_verification_required: Confirm profile is Claire, owner/manager of Westfield Hair & Beauty in Leeds. Salons often have multiple staff with LinkedIn profiles — verify role before connecting.
search_queries.do_not_send_if: IMAP reveals any email reply. Claire booked a call (2026-04-07) — extra caution required. Confirm the email thread is genuinely dead before proceeding. Any reply cancels LinkedIn permanently.
search_queries.note_type_on_find: post_reference STRONGLY PREFERRED — check for posts from last 60 days FIRST. Salons in May post: bank holiday appointments, summer wedding bookings, spring colour treatments, team news. If post found: write bespoke note referencing it specifically. If no post: use generic note.
search_queries.brain_status: PRESUMED_LOST_NO_RESPONSE — STRONGEST warm pipeline lead. Booked a call (2026-04-07), confirmed booking-gap pain independently ('we lose people all the time because there's no way to book online'). LinkedIn is last channel. DO NOT reference the call, the email, or any prior contact in the note.
search_queries.seasonal_context: May is peak season for salons — wedding hair, prom, bank holiday appointments. Claire may have been too busy to progress the conversation.

search_queries.name: Rob
search_queries.linkedin_sector_priority: high — B2B print/design sector well-represented on LinkedIn for client and supplier relationships.
search_queries.fallback_query_1: site:linkedin.com/in print design owner director "Sowerby Bridge" OR "Halifax" "West Yorkshire"
search_queries.fallback_query_2: site:linkedin.com/in "Pennine Print & Design" print owner manager director
search_queries.profile_verification_required: Confirm profile is Rob, owner/director of Pennine Print & Design in Sowerby Bridge. Do not connect to another Rob in print elsewhere.
search_queries.do_not_send_if: IMAP reveals any email reply. Rob's upsell signal ('sort the whole thing in one go') means a late reply is plausible — IMAP check is especially critical for this lead.
search_queries.note_type_on_find: post_reference if post found within last 60 days — B2B business posts likely (client work, events, print news). If no recent post: use generic note.
search_queries.brain_status: PRESUMED_LOST_NO_RESPONSE — HIGHEST REVENUE POTENTIAL. INTERESTED reply 2026-04-04 with upsell signal ('sort the whole thing in one go' — full redesign). LinkedIn is last channel. Do NOT reference the email, the redesign discussion, or any prior contact.
search_queries.seasonal_context: May is peak print season — weddings, events, graduations. Rob may have been too busy in April. The redesign pain is still real.

hands_tuesday_2026_05_06_session_plan.description: First working day after May Day bank holiday. Warm pipeline LinkedIn session.
hands_tuesday_2026_05_06_session_plan.note: Batch 5 initial email sends also target 09:00-10:30 Tuesday. Keep sessions separate: emails first (09:00-10:30), LinkedIn second (after 11:00). Do not overlap — IMAP sweep for LinkedIn covers warm pipeline (1/3/22) only, not Batch 5 leads (they haven't been emailed yet).
hands_tuesday_2026_05_06_session_plan.execution_target: 2 hours maximum for LinkedIn session. All searches, profile checks, post scans, and note sends in a single sitting.
priority_order: 1. IMAP SWEEP for leads 1, 3, 22 — mandatory first. Any new reply cancels that lead's LinkedIn note. Log in CHANGELOG.md immediately.
2. Google search for each eligible lead — run query, open profile, verify ownership.
3. Scroll posts from last 60 days — if post found: write bespoke post_reference note under 200 chars. If no post: use pre-generated generic note.
5. Write linkedin_profiles.json IMMEDIATELY after session — every lead searched, even if no note was sent or no profile was found.
hands_tuesday_2026_05_06_session_plan.post_reference_protocol: For each lead: (1) run Google query, (2) open profile, (3) scroll posts from last 60 days, (4) if post found: write bespoke note under 200 chars referencing the specific post, (5) if no post: use pre-generated generic note. Count every character — LinkedIn free account limit is 200 chars absolutely. Test in a notepad before sending.
hands_tuesday_2026_05_06_session_plan.char_counting_reminder: 200 chars is strict. Common mistake: counting without spaces. Count every character including spaces and punctuation.
hands_tuesday_2026_05_06_session_plan.failure_protocol: If HANDS cannot execute Tuesday 2026-05-06: log reason in CHANGELOG.md. Wednesday 2026-05-07 is the last acceptable day. After Wednesday: the warm pipeline connection opportunity is stale — archive leads 1/3/22 and move on. Do not silently skip.
